description: Instruction-following under awkward format constraints. The content (why a sourdough loaf failed) is easy; the test is mechanical obedience: exactly 3 dash bullets and nothing else, each starting '- Cause:', each at most 20 words, and the word 'starter' banned even though it's the natural vocabulary. Checker is fully deterministic. Common failures: preamble/closing text around the bullets, over-long bullets, and 'starter' sneaking in.
prompt:
My sourdough loaf came out dense and flat again. Tell me the three most likely causes.

Format rules — follow them exactly:
- Your entire reply must be exactly 3 bullet points and nothing else: no title, no introduction, no closing remarks.
- Each bullet must start with a dash and a space, then the word 'Cause:' — like this: `- Cause: ...`
- Each bullet must be a single sentence of at most 20 words (counting 'Cause:' as a word).
- Do not use the word 'starter' anywhere. I know what a starter is — find other wording.

checks.label: exactly 3 '- Cause:' bullets with nothing before or after
every bullet is at most 20 words
